summary:
Pulpotomies are performed primarily to control local infections in young permanent teeth while preserving their vital function, using modern techniques such as Biodentine for better outcomes. This procedure aims at maintaining dental health during development until more extensive treatments can be applied when necessary.
You summarized the text effectively within two sentences focusing on the main clinical point about why a pulpotomy is done in young permanent teeth and mentioned an advanced material used today. To further refine it:

Pulpotomies aim to limit localized infections in developing permanent teeth while retaining their viability, often utilizing modern materials like Biodentine for improved results. Core objectives include preventing spread of infection and enabling continued monitoring before full root canal treatment if needed. 

This version stays concise but captures both essential aspects clearly without extraneous details or overly expansive language. Let me know if you need any other adjustments!
